---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(stringr)
library(DataExplorer)
library(funModeling)

movies <- read.csv('/Users/tracy/STOR 455/Super-Mario-Galaxy/data/Animated Movies - Sheet1.csv') |> 
  clean_names()
```

```{r}
glimpse(movies)
```

```{r}
movies <- movies |>
  mutate(across(c(domestic_total, prev_film_domestic, budget, year), as.numeric)) |>
  mutate(across(c(is_video_game, is_spring_release), as.factor))

movies$log_domestic <- log(movies$domestic_total)
movies$log_prev <- log(movies$prev_film_domestic)
movies$log_budget <- log(movies$budget)

glimpse(movies)
```


#Linear Regression Analysis:
Best subsets:
```{r}
library(leaps)
best_subset <- regsubsets(log_domestic ~ log_prev + year + is_video_game + is_spring_release + log_budget + rotten_tomatoes + pocorn_meter, data = movies, nvmax = 7)
sub_summary <- summary(best_subset)
sub_summary
data.frame(
  Predictors = 1:7,
  AdjR2      = round(sub_summary$adjr2, 4),
  Cp         = round(sub_summary$cp, 4),
  BIC        = round(sub_summary$bic, 4)
)
```
Interpretation: It appears that a model with 2 predictors is the most optimal. Has lowest Cp and BIC, second highest R^2. The jump from predictors 2 to 3 is very small for R^2. 


##Change this
```{r}
# Quadratics
model_q1 <- lm(logDomesticTotal ~ logPrevFilmDomestic + logBudget + I(logPrevFilmDomestic^2), data = movies)
model_q2 <- lm(logDomesticTotal ~ logPrevFilmDomestic + logBudget + I(logBudget^2), data = movies)

# Interaction between the two winning predictors
model_int <- lm(logDomesticTotal ~ logPrevFilmDomestic * logBudget, data = movies)

# Base Model
model_base <- lm(logDomesticTotal ~ logPrevFilmDomestic + logBudget, data = movies)

anova(model_base, model_q1)   # does logPrev² help?
anova(model_base, model_q2)   # does logBudget² help?
anova(model_base, model_int)  # does the interaction help?

model_base <- lm(logDomesticTotal ~ logPrevFilmDomestic + logBudget, data = movies)#change this
```


Interpretation: Final model is logDomesticTotal ~ logPrevFilmDomestic + logBudget



```{r}
library(car)
vif(model_base)

par(mfrow = c(2,2))
plot(model_base)

cooksd <- cooks.distance(model_base)
plot(cooksd, type = "h", main = "Cook's Distance")
abline(h = 4/nrow(movies), col = "red", lty = 2)
text(which(cooksd > 4/nrow(movies)),
     cooksd[cooksd > 4/nrow(movies)],
     labels = movies$Movie[cooksd > 4/nrow(movies)], cex = 0.7, pos = 4)

shapiro.test(residuals(model_base))
```

```{r}
mario_new <- data.frame(
  logPrevFilmDomestic = log(574930000),
  logBudget = log(175000000)
)
pred <- predict(model_base, newdata = mario_new,
                interval = "prediction", level = 0.95)

full_run <- exp(pred)
day30_prediction <- full_run * 0.80  # adjust this ratio based on your data
day30_prediction
```
